_cite.reason: promote enacted Reg 2023/1805 from item's own discovered pool to source_url (retrieval gap, not re-discovery); enables grounding against the actual law

prior.title: Norway Enacts Zero-Emission Requirements for Passenger Ships in World Heritage Fjords (Effective January 1, 2025 for Ships <10,000 GT)
prior.summary: The Norwegian Maritime Authority has enacted mandatory zero-emission requirements for all passenger ships operating in five UNESCO World Heritage fjords (Nærøyfjord, Aurlandsfjord, Geirangerfjord, Sunnylvsfjord, and Tafjord). Ships under 10,000 gross tons must comply from January 1, 2025; larger vessels must comply by January 1, 2032. Biogas is permitted as an alternative, and ships must use shore power where available.
prior.full_brief:
# Norway Zero-Emission Requirements for Passenger Ships in World Heritage Fjords

**Regulatory Fact Document | Severity: ACTION REQUIRED**
*Issued under the Environmental Safety Regulations for Ships and Mobile Offshore Units (FOR-2025-04-10-671)*
*Prepared for freight forwarding operations with ocean transport capability and cruise/tourism sector client exposure*

---

# Purpose and Scope of This Document

This document covers Norwegian regulation FOR-2025-04-10-671, enacted 10 April 2025 by the Norwegian Maritime Authority (NMA) on behalf of the Ministry of Climate and Environment, amending Regulations of 30 May 2012 No. 488 on environmental safety for ships and mobile offshore units. The amendment introduces new §§ 10b and 10c, establishing zero-emission operating requirements for passenger ships in Norway's UNESCO World Heritage fjords.

**Convention notes:** The regulation text is published in Norwegian on Lovdata (FOR-2025-04-10-671). English-language summaries have been issued by the NMA (Circular RSR 04-2025) and the Norwegian Government (regjeringen.no). All binding language in this document is sourced to verbatim spans from the Norwegian or English-language official sources listed. Where English translations of the Norwegian regulatory text are used, they are drawn from official NMA and Government publications. Analysis and interpretive content is labeled throughout.

The regulation applies to passenger ship operators — not freight forwarders as principal obligated parties. However, workspaces providing freight forwarding, logistics coordination, or port agency services to cruise operators, ferry operators, and tourism vessel operators serving the five World Heritage fjords are directly affected through client compliance obligations, vessel access requirements, port call scheduling, and bunker fuel supply chain management.

**Scope of this document:** This brief covers the zero-emission requirement, its phased deadlines, the five covered fjords, compliant fuel types and certification requirements, shore power obligations, exemption mechanics, enforcement, and the regulatory timeline. It does not cover adjacent Norwegian marine regulations beyond what is cross-referenced in FOR-2025-04-10-671 itself.

*Source: Regulations of 30 May 2012 No. 488, as amended by FOR-2025-04-10-671, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

---

# What This Regulation Is and Why It Applies to the Workspace

## What the regulation is

The Norwegian Maritime Authority, acting on behalf of the Ministry of Climate and Environment, has enacted amendments to Norway's Environmental Safety Regulations for Ships that establish a mandatory zero-emission operating requirement for all passenger ships navigating the five UNESCO World Heritage fjords. The requirement is set out in new § 10b of the Environmental Safety Regulations.

**FACT:** "From 1 January 2026, all passenger ships below 10,000 GT must operate on energy sources that produce no direct carbon dioxide (CO₂) or methane (CH₄) emissions while within the World Heritage fjords." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

**FACT:** "From 1 January 2032, the same requirement will extend to ships of 10,000 GT and above." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

**FACT:** "The zero-emission requirement is established under the Environmental Safety Regulations and has been determined by the Government. The Norwegian Maritime Authority initiated this work in 2018." *Source: NMA News, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 15 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/news/zero-emission-requirement-for-the-world-heritage-fjords-adopted/

**FACT:** "Responsibility for compliance rests with the shipping company." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

## The five covered fjords

**FACT:** "The requirements will apply to the five World Heritage fjords: Nærøyfjord, Aurlandsfjord, Geirangerfjord, Sunnylvsfjord, and Tafjord." *Source: Norwegian Government News, Ministry of Climate and Environment, 22 April 2025. https://regjeringen.no/en/whats-new/zero-emission-requirements-for-passenger-ships-in-world-heritage-fjords/id3097475

These five fjords form part of the West Norwegian Fjord Landscape, one of eight Norwegian UNESCO World Heritage sites.

## Why it applies to the workspace

The regulation's direct obligation falls on shipping companies operating passenger vessels in the named fjords. Workspaces in freight forwarding, logistics coordination, and maritime services roles are affected in the following ways:

*Operational implication:* Workspaces providing port agency, logistics coordination, or bunker supply services to cruise operators, ferry operators, or tourist vessel operators calling at Flåm, Geiranger, or other ports within the five fjords must understand that from 1 January 2026, vessels under 10,000 GT face an operating prohibition on CO₂- and CH₄-emitting fuels. Non-compliant vessels cannot lawfully operate in the fjords, which directly affects port call scheduling, itinerary viability, and supply chain planning for client operators.

*Operational implication:* Workspaces handling bunker fuel procurement or coordination for vessel operators calling at World Heritage fjord ports must account for the certification and documentation requirements now imposed on fuels delivered to those vessels — including bunker delivery note requirements aligned with FuelEU Maritime, sustainability certification, and biogas sourcing chain-of-custody documentation.

*Operational implication:* Workspaces involved in shore power infrastructure coordination or vessel-to-port connectivity services for fjord-calling vessels face a new mandatory shore power connection obligation when compatible facilities are available.

*Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether any specific contractual or regulatory obligation attaches to a freight forwarder or port agent (as distinct from the shipping company as the named obligated party) under § 10b requires legal review under Norwegian maritime law and the workspace's specific service agreements.

---

# Issues Requiring Immediate Action

## WINDOW CLOSING — Verify client vessel compliance status for <10,000 GT operators before 1 January 2026 deadline (now in force)

**Note:** The 1 January 2026 deadline for vessels under 10,000 GT is now in force. For workspaces with client operators that have not yet confirmed compliance, the window for orderly transition has closed. Any vessel under 10,000 GT still operating in the fjords on conventional fossil fuels is in breach.

*Operational implication:* Workspaces with live port call or logistics coordination mandates for sub-10,000 GT passenger vessels calling at fjord ports should obtain written confirmation from those operators of their compliance status (zero-emission fuel type, certification documentation, or active exemption from the NMA under § 10c) before accepting or confirming port call bookings.

*Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## ACTION REQUIRED — Confirm biogas chain-of-custody documentation for any biogas-fuelled client vessels

**FACT:** "Ships must carry onboard documentation demonstrating: Compliance with sustainability and GHG reduction criteria[,] Proper certification of fuels in accordance with EU recognised voluntary or national schemes[,] Supporting bunker delivery notes and additional information specified under FuelEU Maritime." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* Workspaces coordinating bunker supply or port calls for biogas-fuelled vessels must ensure bunker delivery documentation satisfies the onboard documentation requirement. Biogas sourced from the gas grid via mass balance certification is explicitly non-compliant.

**FACT:** "Biogas sourced from the gas grid, accompanied by a certificate based on a mass balance system, does not meet the requirements for operating in the World Heritage fjords." *Source: NMA News, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 15 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/news/zero-emission-requirement-for-the-world-heritage-fjords-adopted/

## ACTION REQUIRED — Shore power connection obligations are live for sub-10,000 GT vessels at equipped ports

**FACT:** "In addition, the Government has determined that passenger ships may use biogas as an alternative fuel, and that the use of shore power will be mandatory where such facilities are available." *Source: NMA Circular RSR 04-2025, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 14 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/legislation/circulars/adoption-of-zero-emission-requirements-for-cruise-ships-tourist-boats-and-ferries-in-the-world-heritage-fjords/

*Operational implication:* Workspaces managing berth arrangements or port calls at Flåm (where shore power is being established with NOK 100 million in government funding) and other fjord ports must verify shore power availability status and ensure vessel operators are informed of their connection obligation at equipped berths.

## MONITORING — Exemption window for sub-10,000 GT vessels closes 31 December 2029

**FACT:** "If granted, the exemption may be valid for up to two years at a time but cannot be extended beyond 31 December 2029." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* Client operators operating under an NMA § 10c temporary exemption face a hard sunset. Workspaces with logistics mandates for such vessels should build the December 2029 hard deadline into multi-year service agreements.

## MONITORING — 1 January 2032 deadline approaching for ≥10,000 GT vessels

*Analytical inference:* For workspaces with logistics or port agency relationships with large cruise operators (≥10,000 GT), the 2032 deadline is currently six years out. However, vessel procurement and conversion cycles in the large cruise segment run five to ten years, and itinerary planning for major cruise lines runs two to three years ahead. Engagement with client operators on their 2032 compliance trajectory is appropriate now.

---

# How the Workspace Sits in the Compliance Chain

## The obligated party

**FACT:** "Responsibility for compliance rests with the shipping company." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

The regulation imposes its zero-emission requirement directly on the shipping company operating the passenger vessel. The workspace, in its role as freight forwarder, port agent, or logistics coordinator, is not the named obligated party.

## Where the workspace enters the compliance chain

The workspace's position in the compliance chain depends on the specific service role:

**Bunker supply coordination:** *Operational implication:* Where the workspace coordinates or procures bunker fuel for fjord-calling passenger vessels, it sits upstream of the vessel's onboard documentation obligation. Delivering or arranging delivery of non-compliant fuel (conventional fossil fuel to a sub-10,000 GT vessel from 2026, or any non-compliant fuel type) creates commercial risk — the vessel operator's compliance failure may be traceable to fuel delivery chain breakdowns. Contracts for bunker supply coordination should include representations as to fuel type, certification status, and applicable regulations.

**Port call scheduling and logistics coordination:** *Operational implication:* Where the workspace confirms port calls, berth bookings, or itinerary logistics for passenger vessels operating in the five fjords, it should require operator confirmation of compliance status before confirming arrangements. Arranging a port call for a non-compliant vessel does not itself appear to create regulatory liability on the forwarder under the sourced text, but creates commercial exposure if the vessel is denied entry or subject to enforcement action.

**Shore power brokerage or port agency:** *Operational implication:* Where the workspace provides port agency services at fjord ports with shore power facilities, it should include shore power connection coordination in its standard service scope for passenger vessel calls from 1 January 2026.

*Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether a freight forwarder or port agent acting on behalf of a non-compliant vessel operator incurs any secondary liability under the Ship Safety and Security Act (the enforcement instrument cited for violation fines) requires specific Norwegian legal review.

## Supply chain role by transaction type

| Role | Position in compliance chain | Action |
|---|---|---|
| Bunker fuel coordinator | Upstream of onboard documentation | Require certification documentation from supplier; include compliance warranty in supply contracts |
| Port agent at fjord port | Vessel–port interface | Verify shore power availability; confirm operator compliance status before berth confirmation |
| Logistics coordinator (cruise operator client) | Itinerary and schedule support | Obtain written compliance confirmation; flag 2032 deadline in multi-year agreements |
| Freight forwarder (cargo on passenger/ferry vessel) | Cargo principal | No direct ZEV obligation; commercial exposure if vessel loses access |

---

# Authoritative Guidance Document Analysis

The NMA issued Circular RSR 04-2025 (Series RSR, Number RSR 04-2025, Case Number 2024/72534, dated 14 April 2025) as the primary implementation guidance document. Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026 (5 February 2026) provides the most detailed English-language synthesis of the adopted requirements, cross-referencing the NMA circular and the amendment text. The following applies the key provisions of these documents to the workspace.

## Zero-emission definition — technology-neutral

**FACT:** "The regulation is technology neutral, allowing shipowners flexibility in selecting compliant energy solutions. Zero emission energy sources may include battery electric systems, green hydrogen, green ammonia, or other fuels meeting the regulatory definition." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

**FACT:** "It is up to the operators in the World Heritage fjords to use energy sources that do not emit carbon dioxide (CO₂) and methane (CH₄) during use." *Source: NMA News, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 15 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/news/zero-emission-requirement-for-the-world-heritage-fjords-adopted/

*Operational implication:* For workspaces coordinating bunker supply, the range of compliant energy types is broad — batteries, green hydrogen, green ammonia, and qualifying biogas are all noted as permissible. This means the workspace cannot assume a single fuel type; supply contracts must be fuel-type specific.

## Pilot fuel allowance

**FACT:** "Limited quantities of traditional 'pilot fuels' may still be used where necessary to ignite alternative fuels, such as diesel ignition for ammonia combustion engines." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* Vessels using ammonia or hydrogen internal combustion engines may carry small quantities of conventional ignition fuel. This has a limited but real effect on bunker delivery note documentation — both the primary zero-emission fuel and the pilot fuel must be accurately documented.

## Nitrous oxide (N₂O) mitigation

**FACT:** "Where zero-emission fuel sources generate N₂O, ships must fit and operate best available technology to mitigate these emissions." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* This is a vessel equipment requirement rather than a bunker supply or port agency issue, but workspaces providing technical consultancy or procurement support to vessel operators should flag this as a vessel readiness item for ammonia or hydrogen engine installations.

## Renewable fuels of non-biological origin (RFNBOs) — GHG reduction threshold

**FACT:** "Renewable fuels of non biological origin[:] These must achieve 70% or greater greenhouse gas reduction, measured under EU methodologies. Verification that electricity used in production meets renewable criteria." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

This requirement is cross-referenced to EU Renewable Energy Directive (RED) Article 25(2) as cited in § 10b of FOR-2025-04-10-671.

## Biogas — specific conditions

**FACT:** "When biogas is used as an alternative fuel, it must be kept separate from fossil fuels until it is bunkered, it must be bunkered within the final month before the ship enters the World Heritage fjords, and the amount bunkered must correspond to the amount expected to be used during the voyage though the fjords." *Source: Maritime Executive, 16 April 2025. https://maritime-executive.com/article/norway-adopts-zero-emissions-requirements-for-fjords

**FACT:** "Biogas certification linked to gas grid mass balance systems will not be accepted." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

**FACT:** The biogas "must be bunkered within the final month before the ship enters the World Heritage fjords, and the amount bunkered must correspond to the amount expected to be used during the voyage though the fjords." *Source: NMA News, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 15 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/news/zero-emission-requirement-for-the-world-heritage-fjords-adopted/

*Operational implication:* For workspaces handling bunker logistics for biogas-fuelled vessels, the one-month pre-entry bunkering window and quantity-matching requirement impose a voyage-specific supply chain discipline. Biogas must be sourced and documented to match the specific fjord voyage's expected energy consumption, not on a general account basis.

## GHG reduction thresholds for biogas

**FACT:** "It achieves 50% to 65% GHG reduction depending on production date." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

**FACT:** "It is not produced from food or feed crops." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

## Liquid biofuels — status unclear

**FACT:** "The use of liquid biofuels is unclear. The Norwegian regulations only reference the use of biogas, which is clearly defined in the cross referenced EU renewable energy directive (EU) 2018/2001 regulation 2(28), as meaning 'gaseous fuels produced from biomass'." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Explicit regulatory gap:* The adopted regulation does not clearly resolve whether liquid biofuels (bio-diesel, bio-methanol) qualify as compliant alternatives. The pre-adoption consultation proposed liquid biofuels under Alternative 2 of the NMA's 2024 revised proposal, but the final enacted regulation appears to reference only biogas in the biofuel alternative provisions. *Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether liquid biofuels qualify as compliant energy sources under the enacted § 10b requires legal and regulatory confirmation from the NMA.

## Other alternative fuels (blue hydrogen, etc.)

**FACT:** "Fuel types such as blue hydrogen must be certified under FuelEU Maritime and demonstrate required GHG reductions." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

## No measured emissions verification

**FACT:** "The regulation does not require verification of actual ship emissions. Instead, compliance is presumed if the ship uses an approved energy source that meets the zero emission definition and, where relevant, uses best available technology to reduce nitrous oxide (N₂O). The regulation focuses on fuel properties, not measured output." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* This is a significant operational simplification for vessels and their logistics support chains. The compliance verification burden falls on fuel certification documentation rather than continuous emission measurement or monitoring systems.

## Shore power — mandatory where available

**FACT:** "Shore power systems: shipping companies must also ensure that their ships connect to shore power systems whenever compatible facilities are available to minimise emissions when berthed in fjord ports." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

---

# Anticipated Authoritative Guidance and Pending Regulatory Events

## Shore power infrastructure at Flåm — funding allocated, delivery timing unconfirmed

**FACT:** "The government has therefore allocated NOK 100 million in support to Aurland Port Authority in the 2025 state budget, which Enova has been given a special mandate to administer." *Source: Norwegian Government News, Ministry of Climate and Environment, 22 April 2025. https://regjeringen.no/en/whats-new/zero-emission-requirements-for-passenger-ships-in-world-heritage-fjords/id3097475

*Specific completion date not available from primary sources as of the date of this brief.* The government has allocated funding and mandated Enova to administer it, but no sourced confirmed operational date for the Flåm shore power installation has been identified in the source materials.

*Analytical inference:* Given that the NOK 100 million allocation is in the 2025 state budget and shore power availability is both the trigger for the mandatory connection obligation and the precondition for the § 10c exemption (operators cannot claim exemption once shore power becomes available to them), the shore power commissioning date at Flåm is a material commercial event for vessel operators and their logistics partners. Re-check window: monitor Enova and Aurland Port Authority announcements for commissioning date. Severity: MONITORING.

## Exemption status for individual vessels — ongoing NMA determinations

The NMA is currently receiving and processing § 10c exemption applications from sub-10,000 GT vessel operators that operated in the fjords in 2024. No consolidated exemption decision list has been identified in the source materials. *Specific list of vessels granted exemption not available from primary sources as of the date of this brief.*

*Analytical inference:* Workspaces coordinating port calls for sub-10,000 GT passenger vessels should directly confirm with each operator whether an NMA exemption has been applied for and, if so, whether it has been granted, as unconfirmed exemption status creates scheduling risk.

## 2032 deadline for ≥10,000 GT vessels — no further regulatory development pending in sourced materials

*Analytical inference:* The 1 January 2032 deadline for large passenger ships is set in enacted regulation. No further anticipated regulatory changes affecting this deadline have been identified in the sourced materials. However, the technology supply chain for large vessel zero-emission conversion (green hydrogen, green ammonia bunkering, large-scale battery systems) is still developing. Re-check window: monitor NMA and IMO MEPC regulatory developments on large passenger vessel zero-emission technology availability annually from 2026 through 2031. Severity: MONITORING.

---

# Threshold Questions

## Decided threshold questions

**1. Which vessels are in scope?**
**FACT:** The regulation applies to "passenger ships," defined under SOLAS Chapter I Part A Regulation 2(f) and the Norwegian Regulations on surveys, construction and equipment for passenger ships, as "a vessel which can carry more than 12 passengers." High-speed passenger vessels are included. *Source: NMA Consultation Document, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 7 April 2023. https://www.sdir.no/en/regelverk/hearings/consultation---proposed-implementation-of-zero-emissions-requirement-in-the-world-heritage-fjords-by-2026/

*Analytical inference:* Ferries, cruise ships, tourist boats, and high-speed passenger craft carrying more than 12 passengers and operating in the five named fjords are in scope. Pure cargo vessels are not passenger ships and are not directly in scope of this regulation.

**2. Does the requirement apply to vessels only transiting the fjords, or only those making port calls?**
**FACT:** "The following requirements apply regardless of whether ships operate entirely within the fjords or only transit them." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Operational implication:* Transit-only calls are not exempt. Any passenger vessel entering the fjord geography must comply, not only those making scheduled port calls.

**3. Does the regulation apply to non-Norwegian flagged vessels?**
*Analytical inference:* The regulation applies to vessels operating in Norwegian waters, not by flag state. The NMA consultation document confirms the regulation applies to "cruise ships, tourist boats and ferries" without flag restriction. *Legal Confirmation Required:* The precise flag-state applicability and port state control enforcement mechanism for non-Norwegian flagged vessels operating in the fjords requires legal confirmation, though the NMA's enforcement role under the Ship Safety and Security Act suggests Norwegian port state jurisdiction.

**4. Which fjords are in scope?**
**FACT — Decided:** Nærøyfjord, Aurlandsfjord, Geirangerfjord, Sunnylvsfjord, and Tafjord. *Source: Norwegian Government News, Ministry of Climate and Environment, 22 April 2025. https://regjeringen.no/en/whats-new/zero-emission-requirements-for-passenger-ships-in-world-heritage-fjords/id3097475

## Open threshold questions requiring legal or regulatory confirmation

*Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether the regulation applies to vessels operating on the approach waterways leading to the named fjords (outside the geographic boundaries of the named fjords themselves) — the source texts define scope by named fjord, but do not publish precise geographic coordinates or chart boundaries.

*Legal Confirmation Required:* Whether the liquid biofuel gap (identified in Section 5 above) will be resolved by supplementary NMA guidance or amendment, and what the transitional position is for operators who had planned to use liquid biofuels pending a final ruling.

---

# Substantive Requirements

## Zero-emission fuel requirement — sub-10,000 GT (in force from 1 January 2026)

**FACT:** The regulation provides, in Norwegian: "Fra 1. januar 2026 skal passasjerskip i verdensarvfjordene med bruttotonnasje under 10 000 bruke energikilder som ikke gir direkte utslipp av karbondioksid (CO₂) eller metan (CH₄)." ["From 1 January 2026, passenger ships in the World Heritage fjords with gross tonnage under 10,000 shall use energy sources that do not produce direct emissions of carbon dioxide (CO₂) or methane (CH₄)."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Zero-emission fuel requirement — ≥10,000 GT (from 1 January 2032)

**FACT:** "Fra 1. januar 2032 skal passasjerskip i verdensarvfjordene med bruttotonnasje 10 000 eller mer bruke energikilder som ikke gir direkte utslipp av karbondioksid (CO₂) eller metan (CH₄)." ["From 1 January 2032, passenger ships in the World Heritage fjords with gross tonnage of 10,000 or more shall use energy sources that do not produce direct emissions of carbon dioxide (CO₂) or methane (CH₄)."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Pilot fuel allowance

**FACT:** "Drivstoff som gir direkte utslipp av klimagasser, kan benyttes i den grad det er nødvendig for å antenne energikilder nevnt i første og annet ledd." ["Fuel that produces direct emissions of greenhouse gases may be used to the extent necessary to ignite energy sources referred to in the first and second paragraphs."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## N₂O best-available-technology requirement

**FACT:** "Dersom lystgass (N₂O) dannes ved bruk av energikilder som nevnt i første ledd og annet ledd, skal skipet benytte den best tilgjengelige teknologien for å redusere utslippet." ["If nitrous oxide (N₂O) is formed from the use of energy sources referred to in the first and second paragraphs, the ship shall use the best available technology to reduce emissions."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## RFNBO certification threshold

The regulation, § 10b, cross-references EU RED Article 25(2) for GHG reduction requirements for renewable fuels of non-biological origin. **FACT:** "Renewable fuels of non biological origin[:] These must achieve 70% or greater greenhouse gas reduction, measured under EU methodologies." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

## Other fuel certification

**FACT:** The regulation provides: "Hvor energikilden som benyttes for å oppfylle kravene i første og annet ledd er et annet drivstoff, skal drivstoffet være sertifisert som beskrevet i forordning (EU) 2023/1805 artikkel 10(2)." ["Where the energy source used to meet the requirements of the first and second paragraphs is another fuel, the fuel shall be certified as described in Regulation (EU) 2023/1805 [FuelEU Maritime], Article 10(2)."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Biogas conditions

**FACT:** The regulation states (translated from Norwegian text in § 10b): "Biogass kan benyttes som alternativ til kravene angitt i første og annet ledd. Biogassen skal holdes adskilt fra fossilt drivstoff fram til den bunkres. Biogassen skal bunkres i løpet av den siste måneden før seilas inn i verdensarvfjordene. Biogassen skal tilsvare energimengden som vil brukes i verdensarvfjordene." ["Biogas may be used as an alternative to the requirements in the first and second paragraphs. The biogas shall be kept separate from fossil fuel until it is bunkered. The biogas shall be bunkered within the last month before entering the World Heritage fjords. The biogas shall correspond to the quantity of energy to be used in the World Heritage fjords."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

Additionally, biogas: (a) must meet the sustainability and GHG reduction criteria of EU RED Article 29; and (b) cannot be produced from food or feed crops as defined in EU RED Article 2(40). *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Shore power connection

**FACT:** "I verdensarvfjordene skal passasjerskip bruke landstrøm der det er tilgjengelig." ["In the World Heritage fjords, passenger ships shall use shore power where it is available."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Onboard documentation

**FACT:** "Skipet skal ha dokumentasjon om bord som viser at kravene er oppfylt. Oppfyllelse av bærekraftegenskaper og klimagassreduksjoner for drivstoff skal dokumenteres gjennom sertifisering og dokumentasjon som beskrevet i forordning (EU) 2023/1805 artikkel 10(3)." ["The ship shall carry onboard documentation showing that the requirements are fulfilled. Compliance with sustainability properties and GHG reductions for fuels shall be documented through certification and documentation as described in FuelEU Maritime Regulation Article 10(3)."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

## Enforcement

**FACT:** "Non compliance may result in administrative measures or violation fines under the Ship Safety and Security Act." *Source: Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

*Specific fine amounts not available from primary sources as of the date of this brief.* The Ship Safety and Security Act (Lov 16. februar 2007 nr. 9 om skipssikkerhet) is the enforcement instrument, but quantified penalty amounts are not set out in the sourced materials.

---

# Product-Specific Compliance Status

*No content for this section as of the date of this brief: the workspace does not operate named products that are placed on the Norwegian maritime market as regulated energy sources or vessels. The regulation's product-level requirements (fuel certification, vessel energy systems) apply to shipping company operators and fuel suppliers, not to the workspace in its freight forwarding or logistics coordination role.*

---

# Registration and Reporting Obligations

## No producer registration requirement identified

The regulation does not establish a producer registration or EPR-type registration obligation. The compliance obligations are operational (fuel use, documentation, shore power connection) and are borne by the shipping company operating the vessel.

## Exemption registration — § 10c applications

**FACT:** "Sjøfartsdirektoratet kan etter skriftlig søknad fra rederiet gi dispensasjon fra § 10b første ledd for passasjerskip med bruttotonnasje under 10 000…" ["The Norwegian Maritime Authority may, upon written application from the shipping company, grant an exemption from § 10b first paragraph for passenger ships with gross tonnage under 10,000…"] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10c, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

*Operational implication:* Workspaces acting as port agent for a sub-10,000 GT passenger vessel that has not confirmed compliance should request a copy of any NMA exemption grant from the operator. There is no requirement for the port agent to register separately, but knowing a vessel's exemption status is necessary to provide sound port call coordination.

## Onboard documentation as the compliance demonstration mechanism

As noted in Section 8, the regulation places the documentation burden on the vessel's master/company via onboard fuel certification records. There is no identified public registration database, reporting portal, or periodic reporting obligation to a regulator beyond onboard documentation and port state control inspection. *Specific public reporting portal or format not available from primary sources as of the date of this brief.*

---

# Operational System Requirements

## For workspaces providing bunker fuel logistics or procurement coordination to fjord-calling passenger vessels

**Gap from current baseline:** If the workspace's current bunker coordination processes do not include: (a) fuel type verification against the § 10b compliant categories; (b) biogas chain-of-custody documentation checks (origin, bunkering date, mass balance exclusion); and (c) FuelEU Maritime Article 10(2)/(3) certification review — then a process gap exists that should be closed now for all sub-10,000 GT passenger vessel clients.

*Specific cost of implementing documentation review processes is not publicly available.* The gap is procedural rather than capital-intensive for a logistics coordinator, but requires staff familiarization with FuelEU Maritime Article 10 certification requirements and Norwegian biogas supply chain standards.

**Scope:** All sub-10,000 GT passenger vessel clients calling at Nærøyfjord, Aurlandsfjord, Geirangerfjord, Sunnylvsfjord, or Tafjord ports. From 2032: all passenger vessels regardless of GT.

**Deadline:** Effective now for sub-10,000 GT.

## For workspaces providing port agency services at fjord ports

**Shore power verification:** Build a current inventory of shore power availability at each fjord port into standard pre-call research. The mandatory shore power connection obligation applies where compatible facilities exist. Flåm's shore power is in development (NOK 100 million funding allocated; commissioning date not publicly confirmed). Other fjord ports' shore power status should be verified directly with port authorities.

*Operational implication:* A vessel connecting to shore power at Flåm or another equipped port will require berth time and connection logistics coordination. Standard vessel arrival and departure checklists for fjord port calls should include shore power connection as a standard item.

## For workspaces with client relationships with large cruise operators (≥10,000 GT)

**2032 pipeline intelligence:** *Analytical inference:* Large cruise operators (≥10,000 GT) are now inside the six-year window before their 2032 compliance deadline. The workspace should incorporate a "2032 compliance trajectory" question into standard client relationship reviews with large cruise line clients calling at fjord ports, and flag in multi-year service agreements that vessel access to the fjords after 1 January 2032 is conditional on zero-emission compliance.

---

# Exemptions and Edge Cases

## § 10c temporary exemption — sub-10,000 GT vessels

The regulation establishes a temporary exemption procedure for sub-10,000 GT vessels that cannot comply solely because shore power is unavailable to them.

**FACT:** The conditions for exemption are: "(a) Passasjerskipet har operert i verdensarvfjordene i 2024 og hvert år fram til søknadstidspunktet. [b] Passasjerskipet oppfyller de øvrige særkravene for operasjon i verdensarvfjordene. [c] Rederiet kan dokumentere at nullutslippskravene i § 10b ikke kan oppfylles som følge av manglende innvilgelse av tilgang til landstrøm. [d] Rederiet kan dokumentere en plan for tilgang til landstrøm." ["(a) The passenger ship has operated in the World Heritage fjords in 2024 and every year up to the date of the application. (b) The passenger ship meets the other special requirements for operation in the World Heritage fjords. (c) The shipping company can document that the zero-emission requirements in § 10b cannot be met due to lack of access to shore power. (d) The shipping company can document a plan for access to shore power."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10c, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

**FACT:** "Dispensasjonsadgangen gjelder for det enkelte skip, og kan bare innvilges for inntil to år om gangen, likevel ikke lenger enn til 31. desember 2029." ["The exemption applies to the individual ship and may only be granted for up to two years at a time, but not beyond 31 December 2029."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10c, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

*Operational implication:* The exemption is strictly shore-power-access-based. A vessel that cannot comply because of fuel availability, vessel conversion cost, or itinerary economics does not qualify. The exemption hard-closes 31 December 2029 for all vessels regardless of circumstances.

## Exemptions for heritage/protected vessels

**FACT:** "§ 14f første setning skal lyde: Sjøfartsdirektoratet kan etter skriftlig søknad gi dispensasjon fra kravene i § 10a, § 10b, § 14b og § 14c til skip som er vernet eller fredet av Riksantikvaren." ["The Norwegian Maritime Authority may, upon written application, grant an exemption from the requirements of § 10a, § 10b, § 14b and § 14c to ships that are protected or listed by the Riksantikvaren [Norwegian Directorate for Cultural Heritage]."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 14f, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

*Operational implication:* Historic or heritage-listed vessels may apply for an exemption. This is a narrow provision applicable only to vessels with formal heritage protection status under Norwegian law.

## Exemptions for government-contracted ferry services

**FACT:** The regulation provides (§ 10b): "Passasjerskip som settes inn i rutetrafikk med offentlig oppdragsgiver som følge av planlagt veiarbeid eller akutte beredskapssituasjoner, er unntatt fra denne bestemmelsen. Fylkeskommunale hurtigbåter som opererer på offentlig kontrakt, er unntatt fra denne bestemmelsen." ["Passenger ships placed in scheduled services with a public client as a result of planned roadworks or acute emergency situations are exempt from this provision. County authority high-speed boats operating on public contracts are exempt from this provision."] *Source: FOR-2025-04-10-671, § 10b, Norwegian Maritime Authority/Ministry of Climate and Environment, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671

*Analytical inference:* These are narrow, government-contract-specific exemptions. Standard commercial ferry and cruise operations are not affected by this carve-out. The September 2024 Norwegian Government announcement separately noted "To maintain the county's ferry services, the county's ferry will temporarily be exempt from the zero-emission requirement." *Source: Norwegian Government News, Ministry of Climate and Environment, 30 September 2024. https://www.regjeringen.no/en/whats-new/introducing-zero-emission-requirements-in-the-world-heritage-fjords/id3051481/

## No flag-state exemption identified

No exemption by flag state is identified in any sourced document. The regulation applies based on vessel operation in the fjords, not vessel flag.

---

# Adjacent Industry Research and Alternatives

## Battery electric systems — demonstrated in Geirangerfjord

**FACT:** "In 2022, coastal ship operator Havila ran the first demonstration of a passenger ship operating solely on batteries in Geirangerfjord." *Source: Maritime Executive, 16 April 2025. https://maritime-executive.com/article/norway-adopts-zero-emissions-requirements-for-fjords

*Analytical inference:* Battery-electric operation on sub-10,000 GT passenger vessels is the most commercially mature zero-emission solution for the fjord context, consistent with Norway's extensive electrified ferry fleet. The NMA's own assessment noted that "battery power is a good alternative for vessels under 10,000 GT and that access to shore power is crucial to meeting the zero-emission requirement." *Source: Nautilus International Telegraph, 21 May 2025. https://www.nautilusint.org/en/news-insight/telegraph/norway-introduces-zero-emissions-requirement-from-2026-to-world-heritage-fjords/

## Hydrogen and ammonia — technology accessibility for small vessels limited

**FACT:** "'Use of hydrogen and ammonia technology as an alternative to conventional fuel is not accessible by smaller passenger vessels,' said the NMA." *Source: Nautilus International Telegraph, 21 May 2025. https://www.nautilusint.org/en/news-insight/telegraph/norway-introduces-zero-emissions-requirement-from-2026-to-world-heritage-fjords/

*Analytical inference:* Hydrogen and ammonia are longer-term solutions primarily relevant to the ≥10,000 GT vessel category facing the 2032 deadline, not the sub-10,000 GT vessels for which the 2026 requirement is already in force.

## Large vessel zero-emission concepts

**FACT:** "Competitor Hurtigruten has also released its concept designs for a zero-emission passenger ship." *Source: Maritime Executive, 16 April 2025. https://maritime-executive.com/article/norway-adopts-zero-emissions-requirements-for-fjords

*Analytical inference:* Named operator design activity signals that major Norwegian passenger shipping operators are actively developing zero-emission vessel concepts, which is directionally positive for the 2032 deadline being achievable for ≥10,000 GT operators. These are concept-stage developments, not operational deployments as of the source date.

## P&O Cruises — public compliance position

**FACT:** "P&O Cruises, which operates cruises to Norway's fjords from Southampton, said it complies with all laws and regulations that govern its business, including any future changes enacted by the Norwegian Government." *Source: Nautilus International Telegraph, 21 May 2025. https://www.nautilusint.org/en/news-insight/telegraph/norway-introduces-zero-emissions-requirement-from-2026-to-world-heritage-fjords/

*Industry interpretation (P&O Cruises spokesperson, cited as operator's stated position, not legal authority):* P&O stated: "We are the only major cruise company producing fewer greenhouse gas emissions today than we were in 2011 – despite increasing our capacity by roughly 30% in that time." This is a public positioning statement and should not be treated as confirmation of compliance with the specific § 10b requirements.

## FuelEU Maritime intersection

*Analytical inference:* The Norwegian World Heritage Fjords regulation cross-references FuelEU Maritime (Regulation (EU) 2023/1805) Articles 10(2) and 10(3) for fuel certification. Vessel operators that are also managing FuelEU Maritime compliance for EU waters can potentially align certification and documentation systems across both regulatory frameworks, but the specific fjord requirements (biogas bunkering timing, quantity matching, mass-balance exclusion) are additional to and not substituted by FuelEU Maritime compliance. These are operationally related but not equivalent regulatory regimes.

---

# Confirmed Regulatory Timeline

| Date | Event | Status | What the workspace should have done by this date |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 May 2018 | Norwegian Storting adopts Resolution No. 672 directing zero-emission requirements for cruise ships, tourist boats and ferries in World Heritage fjords by 2026 | In force as of 3 May 2018 | Background only |
| 2005 | West Norwegian Fjords inscribed on UNESCO World Heritage List | Historical | Background only |
| 7 April 2023 | NMA issues consultation on proposed implementation (deadline: 3 October 2023) | Completed | Background only |
| 10 April 2025 | FOR-2025-04-10-671 enacted by NMA on behalf of Ministry of Climate and Environment; published 22 April 2025 | **In force (as of entry into force date)** | Workspace should have assessed client vessel exposure and initiated compliance review |
| **1 January 2026** | **Zero-emission requirement enters force for all passenger ships <10,000 GT in the five World Heritage fjords** | **IN FORCE** | Sub-10,000 GT client vessels must be operating on compliant zero-emission energy (or biogas per conditions) OR must hold an active NMA § 10c exemption; shore power connection at equipped ports is mandatory |
| **31 December 2029** | **Hard sunset for all § 10c temporary exemptions** | Future | Any client vessels operating under NMA exemption must have transitioned to full zero-emission compliance before this date |
| **1 January 2032** | **Zero-emission requirement enters force for passenger ships ≥10,000 GT** | Future | All large passenger vessel clients (≥10,000 GT) must be operating on compliant energy sources for fjord voyages |

*Sources for timeline:*
- *FOR-2025-04-10-671, §§ 10b and 10c, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 10 April 2025. https://lovdata.no/dokument/LTI/forskrift/2025-04-10-671
- *NMA Circular RSR 04-2025, Norwegian Maritime Authority, 14 April 2025. https://www.sdir.no/en/legislation/circulars/adoption-of-zero-emission-requirements-for-cruise-ships-tourist-boats-and-ferries-in-the-world-heritage-fjords/
- *Norwegian Government News, Ministry of Climate and Environment, 22 April 2025. https://regjeringen.no/en/whats-new/zero-emission-requirements-for-passenger-ships-in-world-heritage-fjords/id3097475
- *Lloyd's Register Class News 03/2026, Lloyd's Register, 5 February 2026. https://www.lr.org/en/knowledge/class-news/03-26/

prior.intersection_summary: Overlaps with FuelEU Maritime (EU) 2023/1805 on fuel certification: FOR-2025-04-10-671 § 10b cross-references FuelEU Maritime Articles 10(2) and 10(3) for onboard documentation of fuel sustainability and GHG reduction compliance, meaning vessel operators managing both regimes face a shared certification documentation requirement, but the fjord regulation imposes additional biogas timing and quantity-matching conditions that are not substituted by FuelEU Maritime compliance alone. Also overlaps with EU Renewable Energy Directive (EU) 2018/2001 on RFNBO GHG reduction thresholds (Article 25(2)) and biogas sustainability criteria (Article 29), directly cross-referenced in the Norwegian regulation.
prior.what_it_changes: Fjord port call scheduling for any sub-10,000 GT passenger vessel client now requires confirmed zero-emission fuel compliance or active NMA exemption before berth confirmation.
